Patch Set 6: -Code-Review

Zam, thanks for your comment.  I'd only been looking at the sanity-quota failure (which is intermittent and has a patch just landed to master), and didn't notice the sanityn test_104 failure.

I was looking at the MDS logs to see what the problem is, and it seems that the OBD_RACE() is only being hit once, and that thread is blocking forever waiting for another thread to hit the same race:

 [29277.591669] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl set_param fail_loc=0x8000018a
 [29280.227406] LustreError: 18935:0:(cfs_race()) cfs_race id 18a sleeping
 [29352.393371] Lustre: mdt00_009: service thread pid 18935 was inactive for 72.167 seconds. Watchdog stack traces are limited to 3 per 300 seconds, skipping this one.

Normally this should look like the following case from recovery-small test_67:

 [ 4878.813603] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == recovery-small test 67: connect vs import invalidate race  04:28:46 (1570336126)
 [ 4878.864451] LustreError: 21418:0:(cfs_race()) cfs_race id 531 sleeping
 [ 4881.735224] LustreError: 21441:0:(cfs_race()) cfs_fail_race id 531 waking
[ 4881.737008] LustreError: 21418:0:(cfs_race()) cfs_fail_race id 531 awake: rc=0

The "stack traces are limited" message is incorrect (fixed in LU-12838) so it isn't possible to see the stack trace directly in the console logs, but it is possible to see it in the sysrq-t output:

 mdt00_009       S ffff8a1a113e1040     0 18935      2 0x00000080
 Call Trace:
  schedule+0x29/0x70
  mdt_reint_rename+0x268b/0x2d90 [mdt]
  mdt_reint_rec+0x83/0x210 [mdt]
  mdt_reint_internal+0x7b0/0xba0 [mdt]
  mdt_reint+0x67/0x140 [mdt]
  tgt_request_handle+0x97a/0x1620 [ptlrpc]
  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x256/0xb10 [ptlrpc]
  ptlrpc_main+0xbac/0x1540 [ptlrpc]

commit.subject: LU-12848 tests: link succeded to an ophan remote object
commit.message:
LU-12848 tests: link succeded to an ophan remote object

An open file gets unlinked by rename,
at the same time a cross-mdt link is able to create a name
for a dying object. That causes a file system corruption
seeing as a failed attempt to remove the test dir, also
e2fsck would see an unconnected inode.
